title: Contain noisy neighbors with per-tenant limits
description: Apply per-tenant quotas and fairness so one tenant's heavy load cannot starve the others sharing the pool.
body: Use when tenants share compute, connections, or a database in a pooled model. Do not let unbounded per-tenant usage consume shared capacity. Actions: set per-tenant rate limits and resource quotas; use fair scheduling or separate pools for heavy tenants; monitor per-tenant consumption. Validation signals include one tenant's spike not degrading another tenant's latency in a load test. Risk boundary: an unbounded tenant can turn a shared pool into an outage for everyone, so enforce per-tenant limits and consider promoting a persistently heavy tenant to a dedicated resource.
